2606.17945.title: Small Initialization Matters for Large Language Models
2606.17945.paper_summary: Large language models provide a tractable system for asking how intelligence itself emerges, rather than only how LLMs can be engineered. Although progress is usually attributed to scale, data and architecture, we show that parameter initialization is a gene-like determinant of training and, in particular, of model capacity. Reducing the initialization scale consistently improves pretraining, with the largest gains on reasoning-demanding tasks. We identify two widely used empirical settings that restrain the advantage of small initialization, and show how relaxing them restores favorable scaling. We further uncover a critical initialization that balances the reasoning and training. Mechanistically, small initialization drives a distinct developmental trajectory: parameters first condense into low-complexity structures and later expand into richer representations, giving concrete form to the idea that compression is intelligence. Token-level analyses show that the gains concentrate on non-trivial, context-constrained predictions rather than all tokens uniformly. These results motivate a simple $γ$-initialization rule: expose initialization rage as an explicit knob and use small initialization by default, an almost cost-free intervention that improves pretraining and strengthens reasoning across model scales.
2606.17945.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型提供了一個可行的系統來探討智慧本身是如何產生的，而不僅僅是如何設計LLMs。雖然進展通常歸因於規模、數據和架構，我們顯示參數初始化是一種類似基因的訓練決定因素，特別是在模型容量方面。減少初始化規模持續改善預訓練，對於需要推理的任務獲得的增益最大。我們識別出兩個廣泛使用的經驗設置，這些設置限制了小初始化的優勢，並展示了如何放寬這些限制以恢復有利的擴展。我們進一步發現了一個關鍵的初始化，它平衡了推理和訓練。在機制上，小初始化驅動了一條不同的發展軌跡：參數首先凝聚成低複雜度的結構，然後擴展成更豐富的表示，具體體現了壓縮即智慧的概念。標記級別的分析顯示，增益集中在非平凡的、受限於上下文的預測上，而不是所有標記均勻分佈。這些結果促使我們提出一個簡單的$γ$-初始化規則：將初始化範圍作為一個明確的調節器，並默認使用小初始化，這是一個幾乎無成本的干預，能改善預訓練並加強各模型規模的推理能力。
2606.17945.author: Liangkai Hang et.al.
2606.17945.authors: Liangkai Hang, Junjie Yao, Zhiyu Li, Feiyu Xiong, Hongkang Yang, Zhi-Qin John Xu
